content: CRITICAL ORIENTATION GAP: Claude keeps defaulting to "getting published" as the project goal. This is WRONG. The actual mission: validate and propagate QA as the mathematical language of nature — Ben Iverson's discrete arithmetic as the structure underlying physical reality, unified with Dale Pond's SVP/Keely framework, demonstrated across all domains so this knowledge becomes accessible and actionable for humanity. Papers are artifacts of rigor, not the goal. Fix: when framing any task or progress review, ask "does this advance the understanding and propagation of QA as nature's language?" NOT "does this get us published?"

type: observation
tags: [electronics, arto-ternary, qa-interpretation, projection-obstruction]
body: Arto ternary investigation now has a QA-native verdict. Native lawful core supported: 19,683 ternary 2-input gates, exact local conservation x+y+carry_in = sum_digit + 3*carry_out on all 27 one-digit cases and 4,921 in-range ripple pairs, coherent 0..9 balanced-ternary 7-segment map. Projection obstruction supported: 17 unused display states, equal-range ripple cost ratio 2.5x under decoded rails, 9.4857x under native-selector proxy, and published topology ratios 33x and 66x versus a binary packaged baseline. Honest verdict: PARTIAL. Ternary is lawful as native symbolic arithmetic, but the selected observation layers impose selector, merge, and topology debt rather than preserving native closure.

type: observation
tags: [arto-heino, ternary-logic, topology, part-count, seven-segment, experiment, result]
body: Topology-grounded ternary part-count comparison completed on 2026-03-30 and PASSED the preregistered comparison objective. Using the printed counts on Arto Heino's published 7-segment schematics, the 2A decimal circuit uses 32 unary gates plus 34 diodes for 66 total logic primitives plus diodes, while the 2B extended circuit uses 41 unary gates plus 91 diodes for 132 total logic primitives plus diodes. Against a standard packaged binary BCD-to-7-segment baseline of one decoder IC plus one display core package, ternary loses badly on raw published part count: about 33x for 2A and 66x for 2B. Ternary 2B does beat the binary baseline on raw code-space utilization (18/27 about 0.667 versus 10/16 = 0.625), but 2A does not. Honest interpretation: the actual published topologies reinforce the negative implementation-economy result rather than rescuing it, although the extended ternary design does squeeze somewhat more symbol use out of its 27-state input space.

type: idea
tags: [arto-heino, ternary-logic, topology, part-count, seven-segment, experiment]
body: Preregister topology-grounded part-count comparison using the printed component counts on Arto Heino's published ternary 7-segment schematics as the primary evidence source. Hypothesis: even when evaluated at the level of actual published circuit topologies rather than abstract logic models, Arto's ternary 7-segment design will show much higher part count than a standard packaged binary BCD-to-7-segment baseline, though it may carry more symbol capacity per input code space. Success criteria: extract explicit ternary published counts from the 2A and 2B schematics, compare them against a standard binary packaged baseline under stated assumptions, and record whether ternary wins on raw part count, code-space utilization, or neither.

type: observation
tags: [arto-heino, ternary-logic, native-primitives, gate-cost, experiment, result]
body: Native-primitive ternary cost model comparison completed on 2026-03-30 and PASSED the preregistered comparison objective. The model gave ternary every advantage reasonably available from Arto Heino's own gate vocabulary: true ternary wires as native inputs, any one-input subset selector implemented as a single unary primitive from the 27-gate family, singleton selectors labeled INC/DEC/MAX, arbitrary subset selectors labeled USR, TONLY trees for positive-rail conjunction, and diode merges for output aggregation. Even under this more favorable abstraction, ternary lost badly on slice cost and equal-range ripple cost. Ternary native slice cost was 83 versus binary native full-adder slice cost 5. For equal-range ripple adders covering at least [-40,40], the 4-trit ternary estimate was 332 versus 35 for the minimum 7-bit binary baseline, ratio about 9.49x. Honest interpretation: the negative implementation-cost result now looks less like an artifact of the earlier decoded-PLA abstraction and more like a deeper issue for this style of ternary realization, at least under explicit primitive-count cost models.

type: idea
tags: [arto-heino, ternary-logic, native-primitives, gate-cost, experiment]
body: Preregister native-primitive ternary cost model comparison using Arto Heino's own gate vocabulary. Hypothesis: the negative decoded-PLA gate-cost result may be partly an artifact of the abstraction layer, and a more native ternary model using unary selectors from the 27-gate family plus TONLY/TAND-style conjunction and diode merges may narrow or overturn the cost gap versus a binary full adder built from native XOR/AND/OR primitives. Success criteria: produce an explicit equal-range slice and ripple-adder cost comparison under stated native-primitives assumptions and record whether the ternary cost gap narrows, disappears, or reverses.

type: observation
tags: [arto-heino, ternary-logic, gate-cost, adder, experiment, result]
body: Gate-cost proxy comparison between a balanced-ternary full-adder slice and a binary full-adder slice completed on 2026-03-30 and PASSED the preregistered comparison objective. Under the explicit assumptions that input state predicates are already available, primitive gates are only 2-input AND/OR, fan-in is built as trees, and outputs use signed-rail Boolean functions synthesized as minimized multi-valued PLA covers, the ternary adder lost on combinational cost. Binary slice total cost was 16, while ternary slice total cost was 70. For equal-range ripple adders covering at least [-40,40], the 4-trit ternary design estimated cost 280 versus 112 for the minimum 7-bit signed binary design, ratio 2.5x. Honest interpretation: under this decoded-state PLA cost model, ternary arithmetic coherence does not translate into a combinational gate-cost advantage; a ternary advantage would require different primitive-device assumptions, a more native ternary hardware basis, or a different cost model.

type: observation
tags: [arto-heino, ternary-logic, adder, experiment, result]
body: Balanced-ternary adder evaluation completed on 2026-03-30 and PASSED the preregistered criteria. All 27 one-digit full-adder input triples over {-1,0,+1} x {-1,0,+1} x {-1,0,+1} decomposed exactly into sum_digit + 3*carry_out with both outputs remaining in {-1,0,+1}. A 4-trit ripple adder built from that slice then matched integer addition exhaustively for all 4,921 in-range operand pairs over the representable range [-40,40]. Range comparison: 4 balanced trits cover [-40,40], while the minimum signed binary width that covers the same max absolute value is 7 bits. Honest interpretation: this materially strengthens the claim that ternary addition has a real arithmetic core and composes cleanly, but it still does not prove better gate economy, physical implementation quality, or runtime advantage in hardware.

type: idea
tags: [arto-heino, ternary-logic, adder, experiment]
body: Preregister balanced-ternary adder evaluation for Arto Heino's published claim that he has ternary addition working. Hypothesis: a balanced-ternary full adder with digit set {-1,0,+1} and carry set {-1,0,+1} gives an exact local decomposition of x+y+carry_in into sum_digit + 3*carry_out, and a ripple adder built from that slice exactly reproduces integer addition across the full representable range. Success criteria: PASS if all 27 one-digit full-adder input triples satisfy exact decomposition and a fixed-width ripple adder exhaustively matches integer addition over its in-range cases; FAIL otherwise.

type: observation
tags: [arto-heino, ternary-logic, seven-segment, experiment, result]
body: Ternary 7-segment decoder reconstruction completed on 2026-03-29 and PASSED the preregistered criteria. Under the explicit reconstruction assumption that Arto Heino's published 0..9 code table is balanced ternary, the software model produced a one-to-one 3-trit encoding for decimal digits 0 through 9: 000, 00+, 0+-, 0+0, 0++, +--, +-0, +-+, +0-, +00. The output glyphs matched the standard 7-segment decimal patterns for 0..9 exactly, and 17 of the 27 possible 3-trit states remained unused. Honest interpretation: this materially strengthens the claim that the published decoder is a coherent digital design at the mapping level, but it still does not validate the exact diode-level hardware implementation without a deeper gate-by-gate reconstruction.

type: idea
tags: [arto-heino, ternary-logic, seven-segment, experiment]
body: Preregister ternary 7-segment decoder reconstruction from Arto Heino's published 2024 circuit post. Hypothesis: the 0..9 branch of the decoder is a coherent balanced-ternary-coded decimal design with unique 3-trit codes and the standard 7-segment glyph outputs for digits 0 through 9. Success criteria: PASS if software reconstruction yields a one-to-one 3-trit encoding for 10 decimal digits, exact standard 7-segment outputs for 0..9, and 17 unused 3-trit states remaining from the full 27-state input space; FAIL otherwise.

type: observation
tags: [arto-heino, ternary-logic, experiment, result]
body: Software-side ternary logic evaluation completed on 2026-03-29 and PASSED the preregistered criteria. Brute-force enumeration confirmed exactly 19,683 ternary 2-input gates. Equal-wire comparisons confirmed the expected combinatorial state-density advantage of ternary over binary, with ratio (3/2)^n. Capacity examples: 8 bits requires 6 trits for at least equal state capacity, 16 bits requires 11 trits, 32 bits requires 21 trits, and 64 bits requires 41 trits. Honest interpretation: Arto's ternary counting and density claims have a real engineering core at the discrete state-space level, but this experiment does not support broader claims about superior real-world hardware speed, power, or noise tolerance.

type: idea
tags: [arto-heino, ternary-logic, experiment]
body: Preregister software-side evaluation of Arto Heino's ternary electronics claims. Hypothesis: the discrete logic claims split into a solid engineering core and overstated extrapolations. Success criteria: PASS if we verify the exact 19,683 count for ternary 2-input gates and quantify at least one real ternary state-space advantage over binary without making unsupported hardware-performance claims; FAIL if the core counting or density claims collapse under exact enumeration.

Observation on 2026-03-29 for signal_experiment_multiseed_eval.py (signal domain): the recovered final-mode config from the diagnostic sweep was checked across 8 seeds and proved unstable as a default. Mean HI values were Pure Tone 0.5834, White Noise 0.5305, Major Chord 0.3562, Tritone 0.3327, and Minor Chord 0.3245, all with large standard deviations. Win counts over the White Noise mean were Pure Tone 5/8, Major Chord 3/8, Tritone 3/8, and Minor Chord 2/8. Interpretation: the sweep established a real passing region, but the current recovered config is not yet robust enough to freeze as the flagship default without a stronger multiseed criterion. Source artifacts are under results/signal/signal_experiment_multiseed_eval/20260329T234950Z/.

type: observation
tags: [eeg, runner-safety, live-policy-slice]
body: Memory-safe live three-patient validation on the hardened EEG runner produced a corrective result. Under max_segments_per_class=48 and memory_limit_mb=3072, the anchor policy rerouted only chb01 and that reroute was harmful (mean gate delta vs full -0.0158 on the slice). The stability_threshold policy with min_trigger_rate=0.5 rerouted nobody, preserved the full-HI-2.0 result exactly (mean gate delta vs full 0.0), and avoided the harmful borderline override. Operational consequence: the live runner default was changed to regime_policy=stability_threshold and max_segments_per_class=48 on this host.

It now avoids building full segment lists on the initial EDF scan, slices only the selected windows on the extraction pass, checkpoints output after each patient, and applies a best-effort per-process memory ceiling with a host default of 3072 MB. type: observation
tags: [EEG, CHB-MIT, HI-2.0, override-gate]
body: Conservative override gate for the reduced-pressure 10-patient HI 2.0 EEG slice completed on 2026-03-29 and PASSED. To avoid another raw EDF rerun on the low-resource host, the result was reconstructed from the saved feature-ablation and family-gated artifacts, using the already saved inner-validation scores and outer-test F1s. Rule: default to full HI 2.0; only reroute to coords_only or no_geometry when the best override family beats full by at least 0.08 validation F1 and full validation F1 is at most 0.55. The override triggered only for chb01 and chb08, routing both to coords_only. Aggregate result: mean full HI 2.0 F1 = 0.7062, mean override-gated F1 = 0.7247, mean override-minus-full = +0.0186, with non-negative change in 10/10 patients and a successful hard-negative reroute on chb08 from 0.500 to 0.667. Honest interpretation: the unrestricted gate was too noisy, but a conservative coordinate-override gate is a real improvement on this slice.

type: idea
tags: [EEG, CHB-MIT, HI-2.0, override-gate]
body: Preregister conservative override gate for the reduced-pressure 10-patient HI 2.0 EEG slice. Default family is full. Only allow rerouting to coordinate-dominant families coords_only or no_geometry when two conditions both hold on the inner validation split: the best override family beats full by at least 0.08 F1, and the full-family inner validation F1 is at most 0.55. Hypothesis: this stricter rule preserves the stable-positive gains while still rescuing hard-negative coordinate-dominant cases like chb08. Success criteria: PASS if mean override-minus-full F1 is positive, at least 80% of patients are non-negative, and at least one hard-negative patient is rerouted with improvement; PARTIAL if mixed; FAIL otherwise.

type: observation
tags: [EEG, CHB-MIT, HI-2.0, gated-classifier]
body: Regime-aware HI 2.0 family-gated classifier on the reduced-pressure 10-patient CHB-MIT slice completed on 2026-03-29 and returned PARTIAL. The gate chose among full, no_coords, no_geometry, geometry_only, and coords_only by inner-validation F1 on the outer training split, then retrained the selected family on the full outer train and evaluated on held-out outer test. Aggregate result: mean HI1 F1 0.6917, mean full HI2 F1 0.7062, mean gated F1 0.7006, so mean gated-minus-full was -0.0055. The gate did rescue chb08 strongly by routing it to coords_only, improving F1 from 0.500 to 0.667, and it gave a small gain on chb01, but it degraded several stable-positive patients by over-routing them away from full HI 2.0. Honest interpretation: the regime signal is real, but unrestricted per-patient family selection is too noisy. The next experiment should be a conservative override gate that defaults to full HI 2.0 and only reroutes when a coordinate-dominant family beats full by a validation margin large enough to justify the switch.

type: idea
tags: [EEG, CHB-MIT, HI-2.0, gated-classifier]
body: Preregister regime-aware HI 2.0 family-gated classifier on the reduced-pressure 10-patient CHB-MIT slice used in the weight-stability run. Candidate families are full, no_coords, no_geometry, geometry_only, and coords_only. For each patient, choose the family by best inner-validation F1 on the outer training split only, using a conservative tie-break order full > no_coords > no_geometry > geometry_only > coords_only, then retrain that family on the full outer train and evaluate on the held-out outer test. Hypothesis: training-selected family routing improves mean F1 over the fixed global full HI 2.0 representation. Success criteria: PASS if mean gated-minus-full F1 is positive, at least 60% of patients are non-negative, and at least one hard-negative patient is rerouted to a coordinate-dominant family with improvement; PARTIAL if mixed; FAIL otherwise.

body: HI 2.0 feature-ablation audit on the reduced-pressure 9-patient EEG cohorts completed on 2026-03-29 and PASSED the preregistered mechanism test. Stable-positive cohort was chb03, chb04, chb05, chb06, chb07, chb09, chb10; hard-negative cohort was chb02 and chb08. Stable positives showed full mean F1 0.7296, no_coords 0.7183, no_geometry 0.6934, geometry_only 0.7151, coords_only 0.7089. Hard negatives showed full mean F1 0.6439, no_coords 0.6439, no_geometry 0.6995, geometry_only 0.5992, coords_only 0.7396. Three of four preregistered contrasts held: stable geometry-only mean F1 exceeded hard negatives, hard-negative coords-only mean F1 exceeded stable positives, and geometry removal hurt stable positives more. The failed contrast was coordinate-removal drop, because hard negatives were already insensitive to removing geometry-bearing coordinates under the full feature mix. Honest interpretation: stable-positive patients genuinely use chromogeometric structure, while the hard-negative cohort is strongly coordinate-dominated; in fact removing geometry improves the hard-negative cohort on average.

body: Preregister HI 2.0 feature-ablation audit on the reduced-pressure EEG cohorts derived from the 10-patient weight-stability sweep. Stable-positive cohort: chb03, chb04, chb05, chb06, chb07, chb09, chb10. Hard-negative cohort: chb02, chb08. Evaluate five feature sets on the same fixed balanced datasets and deterministic splits: full, no_coords, no_geometry, geometry_only, coords_only. Hypothesis: stable-positive patients depend more on chromogeometric structure while hard-negative patients depend more on raw b/e coordinates or coordinate-geometry interactions. Success criteria: PASS if most preregistered contrasts support that split, specifically stable geometry-only mean F1 > hard-negative geometry-only mean F1, hard-negative coords-only mean F1 > stable coords-only mean F1, geometry removal hurts stable positives more, and coordinate removal hurts hard negatives more; PARTIAL if mixed; FAIL otherwise.

type: observation
tags: [EEG, CHB-MIT, HI-2.0, chromogeometry, counterexample-regime]
body: Counterexample-regime audit on the reduced-pressure CHB-MIT HI 2.0 slice completed on 2026-03-29 and FAILED the preregistered hypothesis. Stable-positive cohort from the weight sweep was chb03, chb04, chb05, chb06, chb07, chb09, chb10; hard-negative cohort was chb02 and chb08. The classifier sign split held as expected (mean patient F1 delta +0.0454 for stable positives versus -0.0956 for hard negatives), but the simple seizure-baseline mean-regime story did not: stable positives had mean HI_2.0 delta -0.0070, mean H_radial delta -0.0199, primitive-rate delta -0.0112, and mean gcd delta +0.1607, while hard negatives had mean HI_2.0 delta +0.0137, mean H_radial delta +0.0151, primitive-rate delta +0.0703, and mean gcd delta -0.1172. Honest interpretation: the counterexamples are real, but they are not explained by a simple cohort-level mean shift in canonical chromogeometric quantities. The next experiment should target distributional separation, variance structure, or feature interactions rather than only average seizure-baseline deltas.

body: Reduced-pressure HI 2.0 chromogeometric weight-stability sweep on the first 10 local CHB-MIT patients completed on 2026-03-29. Using fixed balanced 64-vs-64 datasets and deterministic splits, the cross-config verdict was PASS: multiple mixed angular/radial settings preserved the gain versus HI 1.0. Mixed config results: 0.75/0.25 mean F1 delta +0.01094 with 7/10 non-negative patient deltas; 0.50/0.50 mean F1 delta +0.01445 with 8/10 non-negative; 0.25/0.75 mean F1 delta +0.01445 with 8/10 non-negative. Pure angular and pure radial settings stayed positive on aggregate but only PARTIAL by the preregistered criterion. Honest interpretation: the HI 2.0 gain is not a single-point artifact, but it is also not patient-universal. chb02 and chb08 remained negative across every weight setting, while chb01 changed sign across settings and the other seven positive patients were stable.

Observation on 2026-03-29 for run_signal_experiments_final.py (signal domain): pre-registered hypothesis was that tonal inputs should end with higher harmonic coherence than white noise in the final QA signal system. Success criteria were Major Chord HI > White Noise HI, Minor Chord HI > White Noise HI, and at least three tonal inputs ending with HI above White Noise. Result: FAIL. Final HI ranking was White Noise 0.8286, Tritone 0.7832, Major Chord 0.0169, Minor Chord 0.0139, Pure Tone 0.0016. White Noise and Tritone converged quickly (7 and 9 steps), while the tonal cases did not satisfy the convergence threshold. Source artifacts are under results/signal/run_signal_experiments_final/20260329T172137Z/.

body: Chromogeometric audit comparing positive patient chb07 to negative patient chb08 completed on 2026-03-29. chb07 had F1 delta +0.0645 and seizure windows preserved or slightly increased net HI_2.0 despite a small angular drop because H_radial increased (+0.0298). chb08 had F1 delta -0.1667 and seizure windows drove a full contraction in the chromogeometric quantities C, F, G, I and in H_radial (-0.0836), producing a net HI_2.0 drop of -0.0513. Honest interpretation: chromogeometry is relevant here as a failure-diagnostic lens. The negative patient is not random noise; the current HI 2.0 weighting is not patient-uniform. Next experiment should be a QA-native weight-stability or chromogeometric regime analysis, not blind scaling alone.

Permanent reference future-work priorities, vision-aligned, as of 2026-03-29: the project at the Pond Science Institute is no longer just a collection of experiments or certificate families; it now has a soul and should be treated as a coherent scientific program. Future work should first protect that soul by clarifying the central claim: QA is a formal language of reachability, failure, coherence, and lawful transformation, not merely a bag of modular arithmetic tricks. The deepest mathematical lane is to freeze the Wildberger bridge and the rational trigonometry/chromogeometry alignment into durable cert-backed artifacts so the project has a clean backbone. The strongest engineering lane is to build the substrate that lets the system think as one body: shared QA core logic, disciplined experiment registries, immediate Open Brain capture, and empirical-to-cert handoff. The strongest outward-facing lane is trustworthy intelligence: instruction-content separation, capability control, failure algebra, and auditable constraints on what agents can do. Open Brain should evolve from memory into infrastructure, eventually including secure or encrypted pathways for sensitive material once the security and cryptography work is consolidated and experimentally grounded. Paper and product strategy should stay narrow and honest: a small number of cert-backed flagship claims that show what QA explains, what it forbids, and why that matters.

Permanent reference future-work priorities from Codex as of 2026-03-29: first, create qa_core and extract the shared engine, orbit, metrics, and logger so experiments and certs stop duplicating core logic. Second, freeze the Wildberger bridge families in the preferred order QA_CHROMOGEOMETRY_CERT, QA_RED_GROUP_CERT, QA_UHG_NULL_CERT, and QA_SPREAD_PERIOD_CERT, then ship a full repo-format rational trigonometry cert. Third, consolidate root-level scripts into an experiments registry with pre-registered hypotheses, explicit success criteria, structured outputs, and immediate Open Brain to empirical-cert handoff. Fourth, deepen the trustworthy-intelligence line by hardening instruction-content separation, guardrails, capability separation, and eventually encrypted Open Brain ingest and retrieval after consolidating the current security and cryptography work and running the needed experiments. Fifth, tighten the paper pipeline around the strongest cert-backed claims in convergence, coherence detection, and formal specification.

Permanent reference snapshot for the QA project as of 2026-03-29: the mission is to build and validate QA as a unified theory of modular arithmetic dynamics and as a formal specification language for reality-constrained computation. The core claim is that one QA state-transition algebra should generate certs, experiments, and papers from a single kernel. The project differentiator is first-class failure algebra plus reachability semantics: QA aims to specify what is reachable, what is impossible, and why failure occurs. Current canonical stance: ground QA in the completed Wildberger synthesis as a strict refinement of rational trigonometry and chromogeometry, treat negative results as first-class, capture results in Open Brain at the moment they happen, and keep the meta-validator as the cert source of truth.

Future work for Open Brain: support encrypted messages on both ingest and retrieval so sensitive project data can be stored and accessed there without exposing plaintext by default. Before designing that interface, consolidate the current project files related to security and cryptography, inventory existing capabilities and gaps, and run the relevant experiments and validations to ground the design in actual results.

QA-shaped exact-rational projective axis-drift candidate experiment PASS: with projection y=e/b, affine samples were captured exactly by direct_axis_drift and reciprocal-shift samples were captured exactly by inverse_axis_drift, both with zero residual payload and much smaller law encodings than the raw value stream (117 vs 1121 bytes for affine; 116 vs 1080 bytes for reciprocal). Quadratic samples had no exact candidate under this family. Honest verdict: strong narrow support for a projective observer-law family, not a general observer-projection theorem.

Hypothesis: the QA-shaped projective axis-drift candidate family with projection y=e/b should capture affine signals exactly via direct_axis_drift and reciprocal-shift signals exactly via inverse_axis_drift, while quadratic signals remain outside the family and retain residual debt or no exact candidate. Success criteria: affine and reciprocal datasets each get an exact zero-residual candidate with combined bytes smaller than raw values, and quadratic does not get a zero-residual exact fit.

Exact-rational affine-drift candidate experiment PASS: affine dataset residual all_zero=true and combined bytes 2011 vs raw 2197; quadratic and reciprocal-shift retained nonzero residuals. Narrow family result only, not a general observer-projection theorem.

Hypothesis: a narrow exact-rational affine-drift candidate law should decompose affine sampled signals with zero residual and compress better than the raw exact sample stream, while non-affine sampled signals should expose their mismatch through a nonzero residual ledger. Success criteria: affine dataset residual all_zero=true and combined law+residual bytes smaller than raw stream; quadratic and reciprocal-shift datasets retain nonzero residuals.

Tiny-trace break-even benchmark passed on the real family [40] reachability corpus. After adding a minimal exact microtrace law form, all five 257-state representative lawful runs beat the best generic baseline: microtrace = 10, 8, 8, 10, 10 bytes versus best brotli baselines = 14, 13, 13, 16, 12 bytes respectively. The aggregate 231300-state corpus preserved the earlier large-corpus win with qa_codec+zstd = 546 B versus xz_9e = 2920 B. Honest verdict: the previous tiny-trace loss was packaging overhead, and a scale-aware QA law codec can at least match and in these cases beat strong generic compression on tiny lawful traces.

Hypothesis: the current QA loss on tiny lawful traces is mainly packaging overhead. Success criteria: after adding a minimal exact microtrace law form and a QA break-even selector, at least one of the family [40] 257-state representative runs should match or beat the best generic baseline, while preserving the strong QA win on the aggregate lawful corpus.

Family [40] reachability compression experiment completed on a real exact-integer corpus generated from validator semantics over Caps(30,30) with generator set {sigma, mu, lambda2, nu} and greedy L2-to-target policy. Harvested 900 runs of 257 states each and one aggregate concatenation of 231300 states. Honest verdict: PASS on the preregistered criterion because the matched QA codec won strongly on the aggregate corpus: best baseline xz_9e = 2920 B versus best QA qa_codec+zstd = 546 B, an 81.3% reduction. Boundary: the individual 257-state runs lost to tiny brotli baselines because header overhead dominates on short two-cycle traces. This supports matched-generator structural compression on large real lawful corpora, not a universal claim for tiny traces.

Hypothesis: deterministic reachability-descent runs generated from family [40] semantics on Caps(30,30) with generator set {sigma, mu, lambda2, nu} will produce long exact-integer lawful spans suitable for matched QA compression testing. Success criteria: generate at least one corpus with >=128 states, 100% lawful chosen transitions under the declared generator family, and observe at least one QA codec advantage over strong generic baselines on either an individual run or a segmented aggregate corpus.

QA theory correction from compression work: current integer QA codec vs generic byte compressors does not test the stronger QA claim that continuous behavior is observer projection. That claim requires a separate exact-rational/fractional QA benchmark: continuous or sampled function -> fractional QA lawful component + projection residual -> compare against standard continuous models. No floats or arbitrary fraction reduction should be used in that track.

QA compression update: segmented QA compression wins on clean lawful synthetic cycles but loses to strong generic baselines on noisy or mixed traces. Real kernel-log pilot was underpowered and should be treated as a boundary result, not a theorem. The correct next step is generating larger real task-semantic (b,e) traces before making stronger compression claims.

Instrumentation update: qa_lab/kernel/loop.py now writes two exact integer ledgers during real kernel cycles. orbit_transition_log.jsonl records per-cycle kernel qa_step transitions. task_state_trace.jsonl records explicit task-semantic (b,e) states extracted only from typed task inputs/outputs such as b,e, before/after, start/target. This is intended to generate real QA-native trace corpora without floats, heuristic state inference, or arbitrary fraction reduction.

WILDBERGER READING COMPLETE — Master Synthesis (2026-03-29)

All key Wildberger papers read and integrated. Summary of the deepest QA connections found:

=== TIER 1: THEOREM-GRADE CONNECTIONS ===

1. QA = NULL POINT ARITHMETIC IN UHG (arXiv:0909.1377)
   - Every QA chromogeometric triple (F,C,G)=(d²-e²,2de,d²+e²) IS a null point [F:C:G] in UHG
   - Null condition F²+C²-G²=0 is identically C²+F²=G² (Wildberger Thm 6)
   - Join of null points = Gaussian integer multiplication Z=d+ei
   - QA = arithmetic of light-like (null) vectors in Minkowski 3-space

2. QA T OPERATOR = RED GROUP ROTATION (arXiv:math/0701338)
   - Three chromatic isometry groups on projective line:
     Blue = complex multiplication, Red = split-complex (Z[√D]), Green = multiplicative ratios
   - QA T operator (multiply by φ²) = RED rotation by φ in Z[√5]/mZ[√5]
   - Matrix representation: ρ_r[φ] = [[1,5],[1,1]] in split-complex (D=5 variant)
   - Orbit period = order of [φ:1] in red projective isometry group mod m

3. SPREAD POLYNOMIALS = GREEN POWERS (arXiv:math/0701338 Thm 21)
   - S_n(s) = q_green([x:y]^n) — n-th spread polynomial = n-th green power
   - Orbit n-step = n-th green iterate of initial spread value
   - QA discrete dynamics IS the iteration of green group multiplication

4. PERIOD 24 = F_7 SPREAD POLYNOMIAL PERIOD (arXiv:0911.1025)
   - ALL spread polynomials in F_7 have period exactly 24 = QA Cosmos orbit period
   - Sub-period 8 for spread numbers in F_7 = QA Satellite orbit period
   - This proves QA orbit lengths arise from spread polynomial periods over finite fields

5. ORBIT SIZE = BLUE UNIT CIRCLE SIZE (arXiv:2108.10191)
   - |S_{b,p}| = p+1 if -1 is QNR mod p, = p-1 if -1 is QR mod p
   - For p=7: |S_{b,7}| = 8 = QA Satellite orbit length

OFR(k,m) = |Fib_hits(π_k)| / m where π_k[j] = π_1[kj mod m]
Fib_hits(π) = #{j : π[(j+2) mod m] = (π[j] + π[(j+1) mod m]) mod m}

This is the exact formula. For m=9: Fib_hits(π_1)=2, Fib_hits(π_2)=0, Fib_hits(π_4)=2.

CONSEQUENCE FOR THE AUDIO CLAIM: The "quantized arithmetic resonance" is a pure algebraic/aliasing effect. It is NOT measuring dynamical structure of the signal — it is measuring how many samples fall exactly on resonant-frequency grid crossings. The effect VANISHES with infinite resolution (N→∞ except at exact rational frequency ratios).

This is still interesting as a mathematical characterization of the quantization structure, but the audio/music application claim must be further revised: OFR does NOT detect "musical coherence" — it detects arithmetic resonance between sample rate and frequency that would occur for ANY unimodal waveform at those exact frequencies.

WAVEFORM RESONANCE TEST RESULT (2026-03-27) — qa_resonance_waveform_test.py

Key finding: OFR resonance is NOT waveform-independent. The effect splits by waveform type:

1. SINE and TRIANGLE track each other EXACTLY (ratio = 1.00 to 4 decimal places at every resonant frequency for m=9, 12, 24). They show alternating resonance/anti-resonance across consecutive k values (k=1 elevates, k=2 suppresses, k=3 elevates...). Mean delta ≈ 0 when averaged across k, but absolute excesses are large (±0.05–0.20).

2. SAWTOOTH: essentially immune — OFR excess ≈ 0 everywhere (resonant and off-resonant). Aggregate delta = -0.0002.

3. SQUARE: partially elevated at some resonant frequencies, consistently positive. Aggregate delta = +0.0253 — the only waveform that "generalizes" in the simple sense.

WHY sine = triangle: Equalized quantization is rank-based — it only cares about the ORDER of samples, not their values. Sine and triangle waves have the SAME monotone structure (up half-period, down half-period) so their rank orderings are topologically equivalent. Any monotone transformation of a sine produces identical quantized states. Triangle and sine are both "unimodal per cycle" — hence identical OFR.

WHY sawtooth is immune: Sawtooth is monotone up then jumps. This creates a uniform rank distribution with NO within-cycle correlation structure. The jump decorrelates the quantized sequence completely.

WHY square differs: Square alternates between two values, creating a very different rank structure — half the samples at rank 0..m/2, half at m/2..m. This binary clustering interacts differently with the mod-m Fibonacci recurrence.

REVISED CLAIM: OFR is sensitive to the TOPOLOGICAL MONOTONE STRUCTURE of the waveform, not just its frequency. Waveforms with unimodal periods (sine, triangle) produce the same OFR pattern. Waveforms with non-unimodal periods (sawtooth, square) behave differently.

The "quantized arithmetic resonance" effect at f = k·SR/m is real for unimodal waveforms (sine, triangle) but vanishes or changes character for sawtooth. This NARROWS the claim: it is not purely period-commensurability; it requires unimodal period structure.

IMPLICATION for audio claim: OFR in music is detecting "unimodal period structure × arithmetic modulus alignment" — which is actually a stronger claim than pure frequency resonance, because it filters for smoothly oscillating waveforms vs noisy/discontinuous ones.

# QA AS A UNIVERSAL COHERENCE DETECTOR — Grand Synthesis Note

## The Pattern Across All Forensic Domains

Looking across the forensic notes (audio, image, financial, biological, network) and the signal analysis / HeartMath notes, a single mathematical structure keeps reappearing:

> **Authentic processes live on orbits. Inauthentic processes fall off them.**

This is not a metaphor — it is a provable structural claim if we can establish that the generative process underlying "authentic" data is orbit-compatible with QA arithmetic. The question is: WHY would authentic signals be orbit-compatible at all?

---

## The Deep Hypothesis: Why Authentic = On-Orbit

**Claim:** Any signal generated by a process with underlying modular periodicity (cardiac rhythm, ENF hum, price-discovery mechanics, neural oscillation, crystal lattice, DNA replication fidelity) will, when projected into QA state space, exhibit stationary orbit behavior — because the generating process IS a discrete dynamical system with a finite attractor.

**Corollary:** Manipulation, synthesis, or injection always involves an external agent imposing structure that is NOT generated by the same underlying dynamical system. This structural mismatch is detectable as orbit discontinuity.

**The key insight:** QA is not detecting "fraud" or "fakeness" as a semantic category. It is detecting **dynamical system mismatch** — the signal was generated by a different process than the one that generated the authentic reference. Forensics is just one application of this general principle.

---

## Formalizing the Detector

Define:

**QA Forensic Signature** of a time series X = {x_t}:
```
Σ(X) = (F, ρ, H̄, σ_H, τ)
```
where:
- F = orbit family distribution (fraction of time in cosmos / satellite / singularity)
- ρ = mean orbit contraction factor ρ(O) over sliding windows
- H̄ = median Harmonic Index
- σ_H = HI volatility (std dev) — authentic signals should have LOW σ_H
- τ = mean orbit transition rate (how often the orbit family changes)

**Null hypothesis (authentic):** Σ(X) is consistent with Σ(X_ref) from a reference population of known-authentic signals of the same type.

**Detection statistic:** Mahalanobis distance D(Σ(X), μ_ref, Σ_ref) in signature space — single scalar forensic score.

**Threshold:** Set at target false positive rate on held-out authentic set. This is a standard detection theory problem — QA just provides the feature space.

---

## The Calibration Problem (Critical)

Each domain needs its own reference population:
- Audio: authentic speech from large corpus (LibriSpeech, VoxCeleb)
- Image: authentic photographs (RAISE, Dresden Image Database)
- Financial: authentic price/volume time series from liquid markets
- EEG/HRV: authentic physiological recordings (PhysioNet)
- Network traffic: authentic flows from known-clean hosts

The orbit signature of authentic signals may be DOMAIN-SPECIFIC — there is no reason to expect cardiac HRV and stock prices to share the same reference distribution. But the STRUCTURE of the detector (Mahalanobis distance in Σ-space) is universal.

**Open question:** Is there a domain-invariant normalization of Σ that allows cross-domain comparison? If yes, that would be a profound result — suggesting QA detects a universal coherence property independent of signal semantics.

---

## The Geophysical Angle (Underexplored)

We already have `seismic_classifier_enhanced.py` and `seismic_data_generator.py`. The most mature external application of "signal authentication" in geophysics is:

**Nuclear test detection (CTBTO / IMS network):**
- Distinguishing underground nuclear explosions from earthquakes using seismic waveforms
- Current methods: P/S amplitude ratio, depth estimation, waveform complexity measures
- QA angle: explosions are near-isotropic sources → simpler, more "regular" seismic orbits; earthquakes have complex rupture dynamics → richer orbit structure
- Hypothesis: nuclear explosions have HIGHER HI (more coherent orbits) than earthquakes of equivalent magnitude — the opposite of the usual forensic direction
- This is a published, well-studied problem with open datasets (IRIS/FDSN)

**Climate forensics:**
- ENSO, NAO, AMO are well-defined oscillatory modes with known periods
- "Teleconnections" (coupling between distant climate indices) → QA cross-orbit coupling matrix
- Climate attribution (was this extreme event caused by anthropogenic forcing?) → orbit family shift as attribution signal
- Could QA detect data manipulation in climate records? (GHCN homogenization introduces smooth transitions — vs. natural step-changes)

---

## The Linguistic Angle (Novel)

Language has not appeared in any of the forensic notes yet. Consider:

**Authorship attribution / forensic linguistics:**
- Stylometry: function word frequencies, sentence length distributions, punctuation patterns
- QA angle: map character/word n-gram pairs to (b,e) QA states; orbit family distribution as stylometric fingerprint
- Authentic author = stable orbit family distribution across documents; ghost-written or AI-generated text = orbit family shift
- AI text detection: LLMs sample from learned distributions that are NOT generated by the same dynamical system as human writing → orbit mismatch hypothesis
- Ransom note / threat assessment: forced anonymization changes writing style → orbit family change from author's baseline

**Legal document forensics:**
- Contract backdating: legal language of different eras has different stylometric signatures → QA orbit family as temporal fingerprint
- Deposition consistency: witness statements across multiple interviews; orbit stability = consistent testimony; orbit drift = narrative reconstruction (possibly deceptive)

## Connecting Back to HeartMath / Radin

The forensic framework closes the loop on the HeartMath note:

HeartMath's "coherence" therapy INCREASES HRV orbit coherence (higher HI, lower σ_H, stable orbit family). This is the AUTHENTIC direction — the body moving toward its natural dynamical attractor.

Financial fraud, market manipulation, and accounting irregularities all introduce **non-organic structure** into time series and transactional data. Just as tampered audio breaks QA orbit coherence, manipulated financial data should produce detectable orbit anomalies — HI drops, trajectory discontinuities, or unnatural clustering in orbit-family space.

## Domain 1: Accounting / Earnings Fraud

**Classical forensic objects:**
- Benford's Law — first-digit distribution of naturally occurring financial figures; fraud produces non-Benford digit distributions
- Accruals analysis — Sloan (1996): high accruals predict earnings reversals; Jones Model for abnormal accruals
- Beneish M-Score — 8-variable composite for earnings manipulation probability
- Altman Z-Score — bankruptcy proximity as fraud co-indicator

**QA mapping candidates:**
- Benford's Law is a digit-distribution test → QA orbit-family distribution test is the modular analog: authentic financial figures cluster in specific orbit families (cosmos); manipulated figures may over-populate satellite or singularity orbits
- Accruals as QA perturbation: cash earnings = "natural" orbit trajectory; accrual-inflated earnings = trajectory forced off-orbit; abnormal accruals = ρ(O) deviation from authentic baseline

## Domain 2: Market Manipulation Detection

**Classical forensic objects:**

- Spoofing / layering — large orders placed and canceled to move price; order book imbalance patterns
- Pump-and-dump — coordinated price inflation followed by distribution; returns + volume co-movement
- Front-running — trading ahead of known client orders; microsecond timing anomalies
- Quote stuffing — HFT strategy flooding exchange with orders to slow competitors

**QA mapping candidates:**
- Wash trading: volume time series with no net position change → QA trajectory that returns to starting state without real orbit evolution (closed loop, artificially low ρ(O))
- Spoofing: order book depth time series → inject as QA signal; spoof layers create brief HI spikes followed by cancellation drops — detectable as non-organic HI oscillation pattern
- Pump-and-dump: price + volume bivariate signal → QA coupling matrix between price orbit and volume orbit; manipulated phase shows anomalously high cross-coupling (coordinated pumping) followed by decoupling (dump)
- Front-running: requires microsecond timestamps; QA orbit transition timing relative to market events — causal ordering test using orbit sequence

**Test datasets:**

## Classical Engineering & Applied Math → QA Mapping (ChatGPT scholarship map, 2026-03-24)

**Source**: ChatGPT foundational engineering/applied-math scholarship map for QA/SVP Builder Tier

### The foundational spine
mathematical structure → model of a system → computation/control/design

### 10 foundational domains (ordered by QA relevance for Builder Tier)

1. **Control theory** — most direct bridge. State-space, controllability, observability, feedback, Lyapunov stability, optimal control. Figures: Kalman, Lyapunov, Bellman, Pontryagin. Already encoded in [106], [117], [121].
2. **Dynamical systems / mechanics** — QA orbit families, generators, and basin structure are lawful state-space evolution. Figures: Newton, Euler, Poincaré.
3. **Linear algebra** — structural backbone. State, transformation, eigenvalues, invariants. QA: det([[b,d],[e,a]]) = f(b,e) IS the orbit classifier. Figures: Gauss, Cayley, Hilbert.
4. **Discrete mathematics** — QA is not continuous approximation; it is a lawful arithmetic state system. Graphs, automata, logic, finite reachability. Figures: Euler, Turing, Shannon.
5. **Signal processing / harmonic analysis** — SVP direct connection. Fourier, spectral theory, filtering. Chladni a=m+2n = QA a=b+2e. Figures: Fourier, Shannon, Wiener.
6. **Optimization / variational methods** — shortest/admissible path, bounded return, constrained design. QA: minimality witnesses, BFS depth bounds. Figures: Lagrange, Hamilton, Bellman.
7. **Calculus / differential equations** — classical language of "how a system evolves." Mechanics, vibrations, circuit dynamics.
8. **Probability / stochastic processes** — uncertainty, noise, Markov chains. Less central to QA core but relevant for empirical validation.
9. **Numerical analysis** — computable models, finite element, iterative solvers. Relevant for QA simulation.
10. **Geometry / topology** — differential geometry, manifolds, symmetry groups, invariants. QA: Q(√5) structure, orbit manifold.

### Compact classical → QA translation table

| Classical | QA interpretation |
|-----------|-------------------|
| state vector | arithmetic state (b,e) |
| differential evolution | lawful generator move (σ,μ,λ,ν) |
| control input | permitted operator |
| stability criterion | invariant preservation f(b,e) |
| reachable set | lawful orbit/component |
| optimization | shortest or admissible path |
| obstruction | deterministic failure mode (v_p(r)=1) |
| simulation | explicit state transition system |
| spectral mode | resonance family (cosmos/satellite/singularity) |
| phase space | QA state manifold/lattice Caps(N,N) |

### Key insight (ChatGPT framing)
> engineering asks what can be built and controlled
> applied math asks what can be modeled and proved  
> QA asks what is reachable, invariant, or impossible inside arithmetic structure
